title: Repair or replace the Z-Axis cable carrier
summary: Restore the Z-Axis cable carrier by either replacing the full carrier or repairing it when only a limited number of links are broken. The source states that the carrier snaps together and can be snapped apart by hand or with a small standard screwdriver, and that dividers between the cable and air line must be positioned fully to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the Z-Axis cable carrier is damaged and needs either repair or full replacement, especially when evaluating whether only a limited number of links are broken and repair is still possible.
not_for: Do not use this runbook when the source does not provide enough detail to safely continue disassembly or reassembly beyond snapping links apart.
Do not use this runbook as a complete removal and installation procedure beyond the source-supported repair-or-replace guidance.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform inspection, snap apart and reassemble carrier links, replace or repair the Z-Axis cable carrier, and verify divider placement between the cable and air line.

safety_notes: Stop if the source does not provide enough detail to safely continue disassembly or reassembly beyond snapping links apart.

system_preconditions: Z-Axis cable carrier is accessible for inspection and repair or replacement.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-17 Cable Carrier diagram showing the Z-Axis cable carrier assembly and divider-related visual support.

steps.title: Put on required PPE
steps.purpose: Prepare safely for maintenance work on the Z-Axis cable carrier.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves before starting the cable carrier repair or replacement work.
steps.expected_result: Required PPE is in place before maintenance begins.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are worn.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE is not worn before beginning work.

actions.label: Don PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on the required PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

source_evidence.text: Put on the required PPE: safety glasses and gloves.
Related procedure details mention required tools including a standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, along with PPE such as safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Gather required tools and replacement part
steps.purpose: Ensure the required items are available before inspection and repair work.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement Z-Axis cable carrier, a standard screwdriver, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, and a 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All required tools, PPE, and replacement part are available for the task.
steps.healthy_condition: Replacement part and listed tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required tools or the replacement cable carrier are unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the required items: replacement Z-Axis cable carrier, standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.

stop_or_escalate_if: Replacement Z-Axis cable carrier is unavailable.

source_evidence.text: Gather the required items: replacement Z-Axis cable carrier, standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.
Related procedure details mention required tools including a standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, along with PPE such as safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Inspect the cable carrier to decide repair versus replacement
steps.purpose: Determine whether the carrier can be repaired or must be fully replaced.
steps.instruction: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier and determine whether the full carrier needs replacement or whether repair is possible because only a limited number of links are broken.
steps.expected_result: A repair-versus-replace decision is made based on the extent of broken links.
steps.healthy_condition: Only a limited number of links are broken and repair remains possible, or a clear need for full replacement is identified.
steps.failure_condition: Damage is beyond a limited number of broken links, or the condition cannot be confidently assessed from the source-supported guidance.

actions.label: Inspect carrier condition
actions.exact_action: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier to determine whether the full carrier needs replacement or whether repair is possible because only a limited number of links are broken.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Z-Axis cable carrier assembly and link arrangement to support inspection of damaged or broken links.

stop_or_escalate_if: Cable carrier damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
The source does not provide enough detail to safely continue.

source_evidence.text: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier to determine whether the full carrier needs replacement or whether repair is possible because only a limited number of links are broken.
Nearby source text states that the cable carrier can be fully replaced or repaired if only a limited number of links are broken.

steps.title: Snap the cable carrier apart as needed
steps.purpose: Open the carrier links to allow repair or replacement work.
steps.instruction: Snap the cable carrier apart by hand, or use a small standard screwdriver to help separate the links as needed.
steps.expected_result: The required carrier links are separated for repair or replacement work.
steps.healthy_condition: Carrier links are separated using the snap-apart method described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: Links cannot be separated using the source-supported method, or further disassembly would require unsupported steps.

actions.label: Separate carrier links
actions.exact_action: Snap the cable carrier apart by hand or use a small standard screwdriver to help separate the links as needed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Carrier link construction and assembly layout while separating links.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source does not provide enough detail to safely continue disassembly beyond snapping links apart.
Carrier links cannot be separated without forcing unsupported disassembly.

source_evidence.text: Snap the cable carrier apart by hand or use a small standard screwdriver to help separate the links as needed.
Nearby source text states that it snaps together and can be snapped apart by hand or with a small standard screwdriver.

steps.title: Repair broken links or replace the full carrier
steps.purpose: Restore the cable carrier to serviceable condition using the source-supported repair or replacement path.
steps.instruction: Replace the full Z-Axis cable carrier or repair the carrier by addressing the broken links, using the replacement part as needed.
steps.expected_result: The cable carrier is restored by either full replacement or repair of broken links.
steps.healthy_condition: Carrier links are reassembled and the carrier is restored to an intact condition.
steps.failure_condition: The carrier cannot be restored with the available replacement part or the damage exceeds the source-supported repair scope.

actions.label: Repair or replace carrier
actions.exact_action: Replace the full Z-Axis cable carrier or repair the carrier by addressing the broken links, using the replacement part as needed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable carrier assembly reference during repair or full replacement.

stop_or_escalate_if: Cable carrier damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
The source does not provide enough detail to safely continue reassembly.

source_evidence.text: Replace the full Z-Axis cable carrier or repair the carrier by addressing the broken links, using the replacement part as needed.
This maintenance diagram is associated with replacement or repair of the Z-Axis cable carrier.
Nearby source text states that the cable carrier can be fully replaced or repaired if only a limited number of links are broken.

steps.title: Check divider presence in every second link
steps.purpose: Verify the divider arrangement that separates the cable from the air line.
steps.instruction: Check the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link. It is acceptable if several dividers are missing.
steps.expected_result: Divider presence is checked across the carrier and missing several dividers is recognized as acceptable per source guidance.
steps.healthy_condition: Dividers are present in every second link where installed, and several missing dividers are treated as acceptable.
steps.failure_condition: Divider arrangement cannot be verified or the cable and air line are not adequately separated.

actions.label: Inspect divider placement frequency
actions.exact_action: Check the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link. It is acceptable if several dividers are missing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Divider locations between the cable and air line in the cable carrier links.

stop_or_escalate_if: Divider arrangement cannot be restored or verified.

source_evidence.text: Check the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link. It is acceptable if several dividers are missing.
The text also states that the Z-Axis cable carrier includes dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link, that several missing dividers are acceptable.

steps.title: Position dividers against the air line
steps.purpose: Prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.
steps.instruction: Position the dividers all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.
steps.expected_result: Dividers are positioned fully to one side against the air line.
steps.healthy_condition: The air line is kept from contacting the FFC by divider placement.
steps.failure_condition: Proper divider positioning cannot be restored or the air line can contact the FFC.

actions.label: Set divider position
actions.exact_action: Position the dividers all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Correct divider position fully to one side against the air line.

stop_or_escalate_if: Proper divider positioning cannot be restored.
The air line still contacts or may contact the FFC.

source_evidence.text: Position the dividers all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.
The text also states that dividers should be positioned fully to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.

steps.title: Visually verify carrier assembly and divider locations
steps.purpose: Use the source figure to confirm the repaired or replaced carrier matches the documented assembly and divider arrangement.
steps.instruction: Observe the cable carrier assembly and divider locations during the work using the source figure showing the Z-Axis cable carrier assembly, example carrier links, and divider position between the cable and air line.
steps.expected_result: The assembly and divider locations visually match the source-supported reference.
steps.healthy_condition: Carrier assembly appears complete and divider locations match the figure guidance.
steps.failure_condition: Assembly or divider locations do not match the source figure, or visual confirmation is not possible.

actions.label: Review maintenance figure
actions.exact_action: Observe the cable carrier assembly and divider locations during the work.

success_criteria: The Z-Axis cable carrier is repaired or replaced.
Dividers are positioned against the air line.
The air line does not contact the FFC.
healthy_conditions: The cable carrier is intact after repair or replacement.
Only source-supported snap-apart and reassembly handling was required.
Dividers between the cable and air line are checked in every second link.
Divider position keeps the air line from contacting the FFC.
failure_conditions: Damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
The source does not provide enough detail to safely continue disassembly or reassembly beyond snapping links apart.
Proper divider positioning cannot be restored.
The air line contacts or may contact the FFC.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if cable carrier damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
Escalate if proper divider positioning cannot be restored.
Stop and escalate if the source does not provide enough detail to safely continue disassembly or reassembly beyond snapping links apart.
